typical_buyer_title: Fire Chief, EMS Director, Deputy Chief of Operations
product_fit: LRAD for evacuation alerts, mass notification, fireground communications
pain_points: Wildfire evacuation notification gaps (Lahaina 2023 failure); mutual aid communications; alerting in smoke/noise; understaffed departments
secondary_funding: State fire safety grants (e.g., MA $5M), EMPG, HSGP (if aligned with state THIRA)
sales_approach: Lead with Lahaina case study - siren system wasn't activated, 100+ deaths. Emphasize IPAWS compatibility for NGWSGP eligibility. Fire departments can apply directly for AFG.
budget_cycle_timing: AFG typically opens Q1-Q2; check FEMA announcements. State grants vary.
decision_process: Fire Chief often has direct procurement authority for equipment under threshold. Larger purchases need city council/board approval.

typical_buyer_title: Police Chief, Sheriff, Commander of Special Operations, Emergency Manager
product_fit: LRAD for crowd management, protest response, tactical operations, critical incident alerts
pain_points: Active shooter notification; crowd control communications; large event security; de-escalation tool (voice before force); suspicious activity deterrence at infrastructure
secondary_funding: State law enforcement grants, DOJ OJP (status uncertain), NSGP (for protecting nonprofit venues)
sales_approach: Position LRAD as de-escalation tool (voice commands before physical force). COPS TEP is equipment-focused. For UASI cities, align with urban threat assessments.
budget_cycle_timing: COPS TEP opens annually. HSGP follows FEMA NOFO cycle (typically spring).
decision_process: Chief/Sheriff sets priorities. Large purchases need city/county commission approval. UASI requires regional coordination.

typical_buyer_title: EM Director, Homeland Security Advisor, SAA Director, Preparedness Coordinator
product_fit: Mass notification platforms, outdoor warning sirens, IPAWS-integrated alert systems, evacuation management software
pain_points: Outdated siren systems; IPAWS compliance gaps (some counties still can't send IPAWS alerts); multi-hazard warning needs; population growth outpacing alert coverage; post-disaster communication
primary_funding: NGWSGP ($40M - most relevant), HSGP (SHSP/UASI), EMPG ($319.5M), HMGP
secondary_funding: SLCGP (cyber-integrated alerts), EDA Disaster Supplemental, state EM grants
sales_approach: EM agencies are the gateway for NGWSGP funds (states apply, pass through to local). Build relationship with State Administrative Agency (SAA) first. Align proposals with THIRA capability gaps.
budget_cycle_timing: NGWSGP and HSGP follow FEMA NOFO cycle. EMPG is ongoing. HMGP is post-disaster triggered.
decision_process: SAA controls pass-through distribution. EM Directors influence allocation. State Homeland Security Advisors set strategic priorities.

typical_buyer_title: Utility GM/Director, Security Manager, SCADA Manager, Operations Director, Resilience Officer
product_fit: LRAD for perimeter security/tampering deterrence at substations, water treatment plants, dams; alert systems for downstream/downwind communities
pain_points: 185+ physical attacks on power grid annually (record 2023); Russian/Iranian cyber attacks on water systems; 70% of transmission lines over 25 years old; 80% of large transformers imported; dam monitoring gaps
primary_funding: DOE GRIP ($10.5B), DOE RMUC ($250M), DOE CESER ($70M), USDA Water Grants
secondary_funding: Dam Safety grants ($33M rehab + $26M state assistance), SLCGP, HMGP, HSGP (critical infrastructure protection)
sales_approach: Lead with physical attack statistics and state-sponsored cyber threat intelligence. Position sand barriers + LRAD as layered deterrence (deter, detect, delay, respond). Dual-use: natural disaster + security.
budget_cycle_timing: DOE programs follow FOA cycles via eXCHANGE portal. USDA accepts year-round for emergency water.
decision_process: Utility boards/commissions approve capital. Security decisions may be delegated. Public utilities have procurement requirements. Private utilities move faster.

typical_buyer_title: Security Director, Facility Manager, Risk Manager, VP of Operations
product_fit: LRAD for perimeter deterrence at chemical plants, data centers, transportation hubs, military installations; alert/PA systems
pain_points: CFATS compliance (chemical facilities); drone threats to substations (Nashville 2024 plot); insider threat; cascading failure risk if single points compromised
primary_funding: HSGP (critical infrastructure protection), DOE programs, DOD procurement, TSGP, PSGP
secondary_funding: CFATS compliance funding, private security budgets, insurance premium reduction
sales_approach: Frame as layered security: sand barriers for delay/hardening + LRAD for detection/deterrence + alert systems for response. Reference DHS Threat Assessment naming China, Russia, Iran targeting infrastructure.
budget_cycle_timing: Federal grants follow NOFO cycles. Private sector uses annual capital budget cycles (Q3-Q4 planning for next year).
decision_process: Corporate security budget + federal grant co-funding. Regulated industries (chemical, nuclear) have compliance drivers.

typical_buyer_title: City Manager, Mayor, County Administrator, Emergency Coordinator, Public Safety Director
product_fit: Community-wide alert systems, outdoor warning sirens, mass notification platforms, event/venue PA systems
pain_points: $40.8B funding gap across 484 climate projects in 124 cities; aging infrastructure; population growth; federal funding uncertainty (BRIC canceled); multi-hazard exposure
primary_funding: NGWSGP ($40M), HSGP (SHSP), EMPG, HMGP, EDA ($1.45B disaster supplemental)
secondary_funding: State community grants, USDA Community Facilities, municipal bonds, insurance incentives
sales_approach: Lead with the $40.8B funding gap statistic. Position Genasys as the bridge between federal funding and local resilience. Help city staff write grant applications aligned with THIRA.
budget_cycle_timing: Municipal budget cycles vary (many July 1 fiscal year). Grant cycles follow federal calendar.
decision_process: City council/commission approval for capital. City manager often champions. Emergency coordinator is technical evaluator.

typical_buyer_title: Event Security Director, Venue Operations Manager, Stadium Security, Convention Center Director
product_fit: LRAD for crowd notification/evacuation, venue PA systems, mass gathering alert platforms
pain_points: Large event security (World Cup 2026, Super Bowl, concerts); active threat notification; crowd evacuation communication; multi-language alert needs; temporary infrastructure for traveling events
primary_funding: HSGP (UASI for host cities), NSGP (nonprofit venues), NY CI Grant (mass gathering sites)
secondary_funding: State event security grants, private venue security budgets, event insurance requirements, DHS event assessment programs
sales_approach: World Cup 2026 (US host cities) is immediate opportunity. Position for stadium/arena permanent installations AND portable event deployments. Multi-language capability is differentiator.
budget_cycle_timing: Event planning cycles 12-18 months out. Venue capital budgets annual. Grant cycles per program.
decision_process: Venue ownership (public vs private) determines procurement path. Public venues follow government procurement. Private venues move faster but may co-fund with grants.

typical_buyer_title: Tribal EM Director, Tribal Council, Tribal Homeland Security Coordinator
product_fit: Alert systems for reservation communities, outdoor warning sirens, mass notification for remote areas
pain_points: Remote/dispersed populations; limited existing infrastructure; unique sovereignty-based procurement; satellite connectivity gaps; wildfire and flood exposure
primary_funding: THSGP ($13.5M), NGWSGP (tribes eligible directly), TCGP ($12.1M cyber)
secondary_funding: EMPG (tribal allocation), BIA programs, USDA Rural Development
sales_approach: Tribes can apply directly for NGWSGP and THSGP (don't need state pass-through). Satellite capabilities especially relevant for remote areas.
budget_cycle_timing: THSGP follows FEMA NOFO cycle. BIA has separate timelines.
decision_process: Tribal Council approval. Sovereignty means different procurement rules. Federal trust responsibility creates unique funding paths.

typical_buyer_title: Installation Commander, Force Protection Officer, Procurement Officer, Base Emergency Manager
product_fit: LRAD for force protection, combat operations, base security, humanitarian/disaster response
pain_points: Base perimeter security; force projection communications; disaster response capability; adversary deterrence
primary_funding: DOD service procurement budgets, DLA contracts, GSA Schedule
secondary_funding: Installation resilience programs, military construction (MILCON), OMA funds
sales_approach: Genasys has existing military relationship ($2.5M in April 2025 orders). Expand through GSA Schedule for broader access. Position for installation resilience + operational deployment.
budget_cycle_timing: DOD procurement follows federal fiscal year. POM cycle for large programs.
decision_process: Procurement officers manage acquisitions. Requirements defined by end users. GSA Schedule simplifies purchasing below thresholds.
